name: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure (Azure)
description: Mapping of the UK National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) Cyber Essentials scheme - Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026) - to Azure checks. Cyber Essentials organises its technical requirements into five control themes: Firewalls, Secure Configuration, Security Update Management, User Access Control, and Malware Protection. Cloud services are explicitly in scope for Cyber Essentials, and this mapping focuses on the controls that the applicant organisation is responsible for implementing on Azure infrastructure (Infrastructure/Platform as a Service). Requirements that apply to end-user devices, on-premises network appliances, or organisational process and are not observable from Azure subscription-level evidence are included for completeness with an empty check list and 'non-applicable' or 'Manual' attributes.

charts.title: Compliance Score by Cyber Essentials Theme

requirements.name: Boundary firewall on every in-scope device
requirements.description: Every device in scope must be protected by a correctly configured firewall or network device with firewall functionality, restricting inbound and outbound network services to those that are secure and necessary.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Use Network Security Groups (or Azure Firewall) on every subnet/NIC and remove rules that allow unrestricted inbound access from the internet to RDP, SSH, and other management or data services.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Change default administrative passwords or disable remote admin access
requirements.description: Default administrative passwords on firewalls and network devices must be changed to a strong, unique password, or remote administrative access must be disabled entirely.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: This requirement applies to on-premises network appliances and home/remote routers, which are outside the scope of Azure control-plane evidence. Manage and document this control as part of your organisation's device estate.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Restrict access to firewall/network device administrative interfaces
requirements.description: Access to the administrative interface used to manage firewall or network device configuration must not be possible from the internet, unless there is a documented business need and the interface is protected by MFA or an IP allow list combined with a managed password approach.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Disable public network access on management-plane resources (storage accounts, Key Vaults) or restrict access to trusted networks/IP ranges, and require MFA for any administrative access exposed to the internet.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Block unauthenticated inbound connections by default
requirements.description: Firewalls and network devices must block unauthenticated inbound connections by default.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Configure Network Security Group rules with a default-deny inbound posture and only allow specific, documented inbound services. Disable public network access on PaaS resources that do not require it.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Inbound firewall rules approved and documented
requirements.description: Inbound firewall rules must be approved and documented by an authorised person, including the business need for the rule.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Maintain a change-approval record (e.g. change tickets or a network rule register) for every inbound Network Security Group rule, including the business justification and approver.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Remove or disable unnecessary firewall rules
requirements.description: Firewall rules that are no longer needed must be removed or disabled.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Periodically review Network Security Group rules and Azure Firewall rule collections, removing or disabling rules that no longer have a documented business need.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Software firewall on devices used on untrusted networks
requirements.description: Devices that connect to untrusted networks, such as public Wi-Fi hotspots, must use a software firewall.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: This is an end-user device control (host-based firewall) and has no Azure control-plane equivalent. Enforce via endpoint management policy.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Remove or disable unnecessary user accounts
requirements.description: Unnecessary user accounts, such as guest accounts and unused administrative accounts, must be removed or disabled.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Restrict guest user invitations and access, and review Microsoft Entra ID and Azure RBAC role assignments to remove unused guest or administrative accounts.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Change default or guessable account passwords
requirements.description: Default or guessable account passwords must be changed before a device or service is used.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Enable Microsoft Entra ID security defaults (or an equivalent Conditional Access baseline) so that default/weak credentials cannot be used for sign-in.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Remove or disable unnecessary software
requirements.description: Unnecessary software, including applications, system utilities and network services, must be removed or disabled.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: This is an operating-system level control for devices and servers and has no direct Azure control-plane equivalent. Maintain an approved software baseline and image hardening process for VM images.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Disable auto-run features
requirements.description: Auto-run features that allow file execution without user authorisation must be disabled.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: This is an operating-system level control and has no Azure control-plane equivalent. Disable AutoRun/AutoPlay via OS configuration or group policy on VM images.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Authenticate users before granting access to organisational data or services
requirements.description: Users must be authenticated before being allowed access to organisational data or services, including cloud services.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Disable anonymous/public access to storage and require authenticated, encrypted (TLS 1.2+) access. Use Azure RBAC for Key Vault data-plane access instead of access policies that allow unauthenticated retrieval.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Device locking and brute-force protection for unlocking credentials
requirements.description: Devices that require a user's physical presence must use an unlocking credential (biometric, password or PIN) of at least 6 characters, protected against brute-force guessing by throttling or lockout after no more than 10 attempts.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Enforce key-based SSH authentication on Linux VMs (disabling password authentication) and enable Microsoft Entra ID security defaults to apply baseline sign-in protections.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: All software licensed and supported
requirements.description: All software on in-scope devices must be licensed and supported by the vendor.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Maintain a software asset inventory confirming each product is licensed and has an active vendor support commitment, including for VM operating systems and any third-party software running on Azure compute resources.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Remove unsupported software
requirements.description: Software must be removed from devices when it becomes unsupported, or isolated into a defined sub-set that prevents all internet traffic.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Track vendor end-of-support dates for operating systems and applications running on Azure VMs, and decommission or isolate workloads before support ends.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Automatic updates enabled where possible
requirements.description: Automatic updates must be enabled on in-scope software where this is possible.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Enable automatic updates on in-scope software where possible: configure automatic guest patching (patchMode 'AutomaticByPlatform') on Azure VMs and VM scale sets, and enable vendor auto-update mechanisms for any third-party software running on those workloads. Note: existing Azure checks do not directly observe whether automatic updates are enabled (Defender auto-provisioning and vulnerability assessment only prove monitoring and assessment coverage), so this requirement must be verified manually until a dedicated check validating update automation exists.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Critical and high-risk updates applied within 14 days
requirements.description: Updates that fix vulnerabilities described by the vendor as critical or high risk, or that address a CVSS v3 base score of 7 or above (or where severity is unspecified), must be applied within 14 days of release.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud system update recommendations and vulnerability assessment, and remediate flagged virtual machines within 14 days of a critical or high-risk update being released.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Process to create and approve user accounts
requirements.description: A documented process must be in place to create and approve user accounts before access is granted.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Maintain a joiner/mover/leaver process with documented approval steps for creating Microsoft Entra ID accounts and assigning Azure RBAC roles.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Authenticate users with unique credentials
requirements.description: Users must be authenticated with unique credentials before being granted access to applications or devices.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Enable Microsoft Entra ID security defaults and prefer Entra ID authentication (over shared keys) for Azure resources such as storage accounts, so every user authenticates with their own unique identity.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Remove or disable user accounts when no longer required
requirements.description: User accounts must be removed or disabled when they are no longer required, for example when a user leaves the organisation or after a defined period of inactivity.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Implement a leaver process and periodic access reviews (e.g. Microsoft Entra ID access reviews) to disable or remove accounts that are no longer required.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Multi-factor authentication for cloud services
requirements.description: Multi-factor authentication must be implemented where available, and authentication to cloud services must always use MFA.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Require multi-factor authentication for all users via Conditional Access policies, covering admin portals, the Azure management API, and users with access to virtual machines.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Separate accounts for administrative activities
requirements.description: Separate accounts must be used to perform administrative activities only, with no email, web browsing or other standard user activity that could expose administrative privileges to avoidable risk.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Limit the number of Global Administrator assignments, avoid granting the User Access Administrator or subscription Owner role broadly, and require named administrators to use dedicated privileged accounts for administrative tasks.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Remove or disable special access privileges when no longer required
requirements.description: Special access privileges must be removed or disabled when they are no longer required, for example when a member of staff changes role.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Periodically review Microsoft Entra ID directory role assignments and Azure RBAC role assignments, removing privileged roles that are no longer needed for a user's current role.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Protect password-based authentication against brute-force attacks
requirements.description: Where authentication is carried out using a password, accounts must be protected against brute-force guessing using MFA, attempt throttling, or account lockout.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Enable Microsoft Entra ID security defaults (which include smart lockout) and require MFA, particularly for privileged accounts, to mitigate brute-force password attacks.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Technical controls to manage password quality
requirements.description: Technical controls must be used to manage the quality of passwords, using MFA, a minimum password length of at least 12 characters with no maximum length, or a minimum of 8 characters combined with a common-password deny list. Regular forced password expiry and complexity requirements should not be enforced.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Enable Microsoft Entra ID security defaults and Microsoft Entra ID Password Protection (banned password list), and require MFA so that password length alone is not the only protection.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Malware protection mechanism active on all in-scope devices
requirements.description: A malware protection mechanism must be active on all devices in scope, using anti-malware software, application allow listing, or application sandboxing.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Ensure endpoint protection is installed on all virtual machines, enable Microsoft Defender for Endpoint integration, and enable Microsoft Defender for Servers.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Anti-malware software configuration
requirements.description: Anti-malware software must be kept up to date in line with vendor recommendations, prevent malware from running, prevent execution of malicious code, and prevent connections to malicious websites.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: Enable Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Microsoft Defender for Servers, and Microsoft Defender for Storage so that signatures stay current and malicious files, code execution and connections are blocked.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E

requirements.name: Application allow listing
requirements.description: Where used as an alternative to anti-malware software, only approved applications restricted by code signing must be allowed to execute, with a maintained list of approved applications and no execution of unsigned or invalidly signed applications.

attributes.RemediationProcedure: This is an end-user device control implemented through application control policies (e.g. Microsoft Defender Application Control) and has no Azure subscription-level control-plane equivalent.
attributes.References: NCSC Cyber Essentials: Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.3 (April 2026), Section E
